skill.name: home-assistant-manager
skill.description: Manage Home Assistant configuration, deployments, validation, automation testing, logs, and Lovelace dashboards through SSH, hass-cli, git, and scp workflows.
skill.summary: Guide safe Home Assistant configuration changes, automation checks, remote deployments, and Lovelace dashboard updates.
skill.author: komal-SkyNET

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found no executable source code or prompt-injection attempt. Most command findings are Markdown examples, while the skill still legitimately directs SSH, scp, hass-cli, token, and Home Assistant storage workflows that can change a live instance.

medium_findings.title: Remote Home Assistant Command Workflows
medium_findings.description: The static command findings are mostly Markdown backticks, not embedded Ruby execution. However, the skill intentionally instructs use of SSH, hass-cli, git, and scp against a Home Assistant host, which can restart services, alter automations, and change live configuration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files clearly document remote operational commands and deployment steps. The intent appears administrative rather than malicious, so the risk is impact from misuse or overbroad permission.

medium_findings.title: Home Assistant Token and API Key Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill expects HASS_SERVER and HASS_TOKEN environment variables and shows an API key header for Context7 setup. These are legitimate prerequisites, but mishandling them can expose Home Assistant or documentation service credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly discuss token-backed environment variables and an API key header. I found no evidence that the skill exfiltrates those secrets.

medium_findings.title: Direct Modification of Home Assistant Storage Files
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends editing and copying files under .storage for Lovelace dashboards. This is a normal Home Assistant workflow in the skill context, but direct writes to hidden storage can corrupt dashboards or bypass safer UI validation if applied incorrectly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden storage paths and scp deployment steps are explicit. The risk is operational integrity, not confirmed malicious filesystem access.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, Ruby backtick execution, and system reconnaissance alerts are not confirmed as malicious code. They are caused by Markdown examples, common Home Assistant command names, inline code formatting, and documentation text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review found documentation content and command snippets rather than executable package code. No prompt-injection text or obfuscated payload was found in README.md or SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: External Documentation and Download URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to GitHub, Home Assistant documentation, Context7, and support pages. They are expected documentation links, but users should review external downloads before running install commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible and tied to installation, documentation, or community resources. I found no evidence of hidden redirects or suspicious endpoints.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Shell and Service Control Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes examples for SSH command execution and Home Assistant service reload or restart operations. These are powerful but expected for this administrative skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present in documented workflows. They target a user-controlled Home Assistant host, not an unexplained third-party endpoint.

dangerous_patterns.title: scp Deployment to Configuration Directories
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends copying automation and dashboard files into /config and /config/.storage on the Home Assistant host. This can quickly change live behavior if used without validation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The deployment pattern is explicit and repeated. It is a legitimate rapid-iteration pattern with meaningful operational risk.

content.value_statement: Home Assistant changes can break automations, dashboards, or remote access. This skill guides validation, deployment, reloads, logs, and dashboard updates with repeatable workflows.
actual_capabilities: Plan Home Assistant configuration changes with validation and deployment steps.
Choose between reload and restart workflows based on the changed component.
Use hass-cli and SSH patterns to inspect states, services, and logs.
Guide automation testing with manual triggers and post-deployment log checks.
Develop Lovelace dashboard files, registrations, layouts, and card choices.
Debug templates, dashboard syntax, and common Home Assistant error patterns.
limitations: Requires user-provided Home Assistant access, SSH setup, and hass-cli configuration.
Can affect live automations and dashboards if commands are approved without review.
Does not replace Home Assistant backups, access controls, or change management.
Examples assume common Home Assistant paths and may need adaptation.

use_cases.title: Deploy safer automation changes
use_cases.description: Validate, deploy, reload, trigger, and inspect logs before committing automation changes.

use_cases.title: Build tablet dashboards
use_cases.description: Create Lovelace layouts with touch-friendly cards, correct registration, and rapid preview cycles.

use_cases.title: Troubleshoot Home Assistant errors
use_cases.description: Investigate template failures, service errors, dashboard issues, and entity state problems.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a planned change
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review before editing Home Assistant files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my planned Home Assistant change. Identify whether it needs a reload or restart, what to validate first, and how to verify success.

prompt_templates.title: Create and test an automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Home Assistant automation for this behavior: [describe behavior]. Include the deployment steps, manual trigger test, and log checks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Lovelace dashboard
prompt_templates.scenario: Dashboard layout and card selection for a target device.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Lovelace dashboard for a [device size] screen with these controls: [list controls]. Include registration, preview, and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Debug a failed deployment
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced troubleshooting after a failed reload or broken dashboard.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Home Assistant error and propose a fix: [paste error]. Use the skill workflow to isolate the cause, patch safely, and verify recovery.

output_examples.output: A deployment plan that validates the configuration, reloads automations, manually triggers the automation, checks logs, and commits only after success.

output_examples.input: Make an 11-inch tablet dashboard for lights and climate.
output_examples.output: A dashboard plan with three-column layout guidance, touch-friendly card choices, registration steps, preview instructions, and device testing checks.

output_examples.input: Fix a template type error in an automation.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting sequence that locates the failing template, applies numeric filters, redeploys the change, and verifies clean logs.

best_practices: Run configuration validation before restart or reload operations.
Use rapid scp iteration for tests, then commit stable changes to git.
Verify every change through state checks, manual triggers, or log review.
anti_patterns: Restarting Home Assistant when a targeted reload is enough.
Editing hidden dashboard storage without backups or syntax validation.
Committing untested automations before checking behavior and logs.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to Home Assistant by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflows that depend on your approved SSH, hass-cli, and environment setup.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is legitimate, but it guides commands that can change a live Home Assistant instance.

faq.question: Can it build Lovelace dashboards?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes dashboard file, registration, card selection, preview, and tablet layout guidance.

faq.question: Does it require a full Home Assistant restart?
faq.answer: Not always. It helps choose reloads for supported changes and restarts only when needed.

faq.question: What credentials does it expect?
faq.answer: It expects user-managed Home Assistant access, such as SSH keys and hass-cli token environment variables.

faq.question: Can it replace backups?
faq.answer: No. Keep Home Assistant backups and review commands before applying changes.
